theme: Route packets without causing a jam storm
title: Jam Storm: Anticipatory Packet Routing
strategies: static cheapest-link routing: pick the one link with the lowest base (zero-load) latency and send every packet, every round, through it (ignores load entirely; the evaluator's own weak reference)

myopic least-latency routing: each round, look at the link states as of the START of the round (before this round's own packets land), pick the single currently-cheapest link, and dump the ENTIRE round onto it -- blind to the pile-on it is itself causing; triggers herding and queue-carry congestion collapse on symmetric / bursty rounds

marginal-cost water-filling: within each round, insert packets one at a time onto whichever link currently has the lowest MARGINAL delay increase (recomputing after every insertion), so a policy internalizes the load it is concurrently committing and spreads bursts instead of herding
multi-round lookahead / dynamic programming over the queue-carry trajectory to shave the remaining gap to the (unreachable) continuous water-filling floor (open headroom)
